Olfactory epithelium contains the organs that sense static equilibrium 6. Filiform papillae jelly-like mass that provides stability and structure to the eye 7. Semicircular canals provide friction, contains no taste buds 8. Retina contains approximately 5 taste buds 9. Auditory ossicles (malleus, incus, and stapes) the smallest bones in the body; transmits vibrations that are key to hearing 10. Circumvallate papillae possess the following layers to allow for the transmission of stimuli to the optic nerve; pigmented layer, photoreceptor layer, outer synaptic layer, bipolar cell layer, inner synaptic layer, ganglion layer 11.
